channel.title: Publications on SBMARUF
channel.description: Recent content in Publications on SBMARUF

item.title: Stop Taking Tokenizers for Granted: They Are Core Design Decisions in Large Language Models
item.description: Reframing tokenization as a core modeling decision in LLMs rather than a preprocessing step, arguing for context-aware tokenizer and model co-design.

item.title: Amazon Nova 2: Multimodal Reasoning and Generation Models
item.description: A family of multimodal reasoning and generation models from Amazon AGI.

item.title: ZeroSumEval: An Extensible Framework for Scaling LLM Evaluation with Inter-Model Competition
item.description: An extensible framework for scaling LLM evaluation through inter-model competition.

item.title: ALLaM: Large Language Models for Arabic and English
item.description: A sovereign bilingual LLM for Arabic and English, achieving state-of-the-art Arabic performance, integrated into IBM Watsonx and Microsoft Azure.

item.title: Beyond Fertility: STRR as a Metric for Multilingual Tokenization Evaluation
item.description: A new metric for multilingual tokenization evaluation that goes beyond fertility-based measures.

item.title: Humanity's Last Exam
item.description: A large-scale collaborative benchmark designed to test the limits of frontier AI systems.

item.title: A Systematic Survey and Critical Review on Evaluating Large Language Models: Challenges, Limitations, and Recommendations
item.description: A systematic survey and critical review on evaluating large language models with recommendations for more rigorous evaluation.

item.title: BenLLM-Eval: A Comprehensive Evaluation into the Potentials and Pitfalls of Large Language Models on Bengali NLP
item.description: Comprehensive evaluation of LLMs on Bengali NLP, examining potentials and pitfalls for low-resource language processing.

item.title: When Benchmarks are Targets: Revealing the Sensitivity of Large Language Model Leaderboards
item.description: Revealing the sensitivity of LLM leaderboards to evaluation choices, showing that rankings can shift significantly with minor methodological changes.

item.title: Transfer Learning for Language Model Adaptation
item.description: PhD thesis on transfer learning for language model adaptation, covering multilingual generalization and scalable training dynamics.

item.title: A Systematic Study and Comprehensive Evaluation of ChatGPT on Benchmark Datasets
item.description: The paper comprehensively evaluates ChatGPT’s performance on various academic tasks, covering 140 tasks across diverse fields, highlighting strengths and weaknesses, and introducing a new ability to follow multi-query instructions, ultimately paving the way for practical applications of ChatGPT-like models.

item.title: BLOOM+1: Adding Language Support to BLOOM for Zero-Shot Prompting
item.description: Adding new language support to the BLOOM multilingual language model for zero-shot prompting.

item.title: xCodeEval: A Large Scale Multilingual Multitask Benchmark for Code Understanding, Generation, Translation and Retrieval
item.description: We introduce xCodeEval, the largest executable multilingual multitask benchmark to date consisting of 25M document-level coding examples from about 7.5 K unique problems covering up to 17 programming languages with execution-level parallelism.

item.title: Crosslingual Generalization through Multitask Finetuning
item.description: Shows multitask multilingual generalization in language model.

item.title: SPT: Semi-Parametric Prompt Tuning for Multitask Prompted Learning
item.description: A semi-parametric prompt tuning method improving multitask generalization for parameter-efficient fine-tuning with cross-task zero-shot generalization.

item.title: What Language Model to Train if You Have One Million GPU Hours?
item.description: Investigating scaling laws and practical guidance for training language models under constrained compute budgets.

item.title: What Language Model to Train if You Have One Million GPU Hours?
PromptSource: An Integrated Development Environment and Repository for Natural Language Prompts
item.description: Over 2,000 prompts for roughly 170 datasets are available through PromptSource framework.

item.title: Multitask Prompted Training Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization
item.description: T0 shows zero-shot task generalization on English natural language prompts, outperforming GPT-3 on many tasks, while being 16x smaller!

item.title: Nearest Neighbour Few-Shot Learning for Cross-lingual Classification
item.description: We propose a trasductive approach for few shot cross-lingual classification.

item.title: AugVic: Exploiting BiText Vicinity for Low-Resource NMT
item.description: We propose AugVic, a data augmentation framework for sequence to sequence model (i.e. NMT) using Language Model.

item.title: UXLA: A Robust Unsupervised Data Augmentation Framework for Zero-Resouce Cross-Lingual NLP
item.description: We propose UXLA, a novel data augmentation framework for self-supervised learning in zero-resource transfer learning scenarios.

item.title: LNMAP: Departures from Isomorphic Assumption in Bilingual Lexicon Induction Through Non-Linear Mapping in Latent Space
item.description: We propose a novel semi-supervised method to learn cross-lingual word embeddings for BLI.

item.title: Zero-Resource Cross-Lingual Named Entity Recognition
item.description: We propose a superior model and training method for zero resource transfer of Cross-lingual Named Entity Recognition.

item.title: A Unified Linear-Time Framework for Sentence-Level Discourse Parsing
Data Analytics: Concepts, Techniques and Applications
item.description: This book chapter goes through various aspects of regression and maths behind them.
